title: fraxinus excelsior
intro: Fraxinus excelsior, also known as the ash tree, is a deciduous tree species native to Europe, western Asia, and northern Africa. The bark and leaves of Fraxinus excelsior have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus excelsior include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Herbal preparations of Fraxinus excelsior include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various ailments, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
